---
title: "Madagascar Influenza — Phylogenetic Analysis"
subtitle: "IQ-TREE ML trees · Monophyletic clade analysis"
author: "Camila Duitama"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    output-file: "trees.html"
    output-dir: "../docs"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

RESULTS_DIR = Path("../results")
TREES_IMG   = Path("../docs/trees_img")  # absolute path used for globbing; src uses basename
TREES_DIR   = Path("../trees")

trees_sum    = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_summary.tsv",       sep="\t")
trees_det    = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "clade_details.tsv",        sep="\t")
reassort_mat = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "reassortment_matrix.tsv",  sep="\t", dtype=str)
reassort_sum = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "reassortment_summary.tsv", sep="\t", dtype=str)

PALETTE = px.colors.qualitative.Bold

def seg_type(name):
    if name.startswith("HA_"):  return "HA (surface)"
    if name.startswith("NA_"):  return "NA (surface)"
    return "Internal segment"

trees_sum["type"]    = trees_sum["tree"].apply(seg_type)
trees_det["type"]    = trees_det["tree"].apply(seg_type)
trees_sum["subtype"] = trees_sum["tree"].str.replace(r"^(HA_|NA_)", "", regex=True)
```

← [Back to main report](index.html)

---

## Pipeline

**16 maximum-likelihood trees** (IQ-TREE 3.1.0, HKY+G, seed 42) across all segment × subtype
combinations with ≥ 10 Madagascar and ≥ 20 African sequences.
**Question:** do Madagascar sequences form monophyletic clades (local transmission) or are they
singletons (sporadic importation)?

| Category | Segments | Strategy | Trees |
|----------|----------|----------|-------|
| HA per-subtype | HA | One tree per HA subtype | 5 |
| NA per-subtype | NA | One tree per NA subtype | 5 |
| Internal merged | PB2, PB1, PA, NP, MP, NS | All subtypes merged per segment | 6 |
| **Total** | | | **16** |

Internal segments are merged across subtypes (they reassort freely and do not cluster by HxNy).
HA/NA are kept per-subtype (distinct antigenic lineages with long inter-subtype branches).

### Subsampling design

| Input | Strategy |
|-------|----------|
| Madagascar sequences | All kept |
| Africa (non-Madagascar) | Proportionally sampled by country to **4 × n_Madagascar** |

4× subsampling reflects Africa's diversity without overwhelming the tree. Trees midpoint-rooted before analysis.

### Clade detection

A **monophyletic Madagascar clade** = maximal subtree (≥ 2 tips) whose leaves are exclusively Madagascar sequences (`scripts/analyse_trees.py`, ete3).

| Metric | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `internal_bl` | Sum of branch lengths within the clade — within-Madagascar diversity |
| `stem_bl` | Branch from clade root to parent — divergence from nearest African ancestor |

### Tree inventory

```{python}
#| label: tbl-tree-inventory

# Read IQ-TREE log sizes to get actual input counts per tree
display_cols = {
    "tree":              "Tree",
    "n_mdg_tips":        "Mdg tips",
    "n_africa_tips":     "Africa tips",
    "n_mdg_clades":      "Clades",
    "n_mdg_singletons":  "Singletons",
    "pct_mdg_in_clades": "% Mdg in clades",
    "mean_clade_size":   "Mean clade size",
    "mean_stem_bl":      "Mean stem BL",
}
trees_sum.rename(columns=display_cols)[list(display_cols.values())].to_html(
    index=False, classes="table table-sm table-striped table-bordered", border=0
)
```

---

## Clade analysis results

### Clades vs. singletons per tree

```{python}
#| label: fig-clades-bar

bar_df = trees_sum[["tree", "n_mdg_clades", "n_mdg_singletons", "type"]].copy()
bar_df = bar_df.sort_values(["type", "tree"])
bar_long = bar_df.melt(
    id_vars=["tree", "type"],
    value_vars=["n_mdg_clades", "n_mdg_singletons"],
    var_name="Category", value_name="Count",
)
bar_long["Category"] = bar_long["Category"].map(
    {"n_mdg_clades": "Clades (≥2 tips)", "n_mdg_singletons": "Singletons"}
)

fig = px.bar(
    bar_long,
    x="Count", y="tree",
    color="Category",
    facet_col="type",
    orientation="h",
    barmode="stack",
    color_discrete_map={"Clades (≥2 tips)": PALETTE[2], "Singletons": PALETTE[5]},
    labels={"tree": "Tree", "Count": "Madagascar sequences", "Category": ""},
    title="Monophyletic Madagascar clades vs. singletons per tree",
    category_orders={"type": ["HA (surface)", "NA (surface)", "Internal segment"]},
)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.for_each_annotation(lambda a: a.update(text=a.text.split("=")[-1]))
fig.show()
```

### Stem branch length vs. clade size

```{python}
#| label: fig-scatter

fig = px.scatter(
    trees_sum,
    x="mean_stem_bl",
    y="mean_clade_size",
    size="n_mdg_clades",
    color="type",
    text="tree",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={
        "mean_stem_bl":    "Mean stem branch length",
        "mean_clade_size": "Mean clade size (tips)",
        "n_mdg_clades":    "Number of clades",
        "type":            "Segment type",
    },
    title="Stem BL vs. clade size — bubble size = number of clades",
    size_max=30,
)
fig.update_traces(textposition="top center", textfont_size=9)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.show()
```

### Clade size distribution across trees

```{python}
#| label: fig-clade-sizes

fig = px.box(
    trees_det,
    x="tree", y="n_tips",
    color="type",
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    points="outliers",
    labels={"n_tips": "Clade size (tips)", "tree": "Tree", "type": "Segment type"},
    title="Distribution of Madagascar clade sizes across all 16 trees",
    category_orders={"type": ["HA (surface)", "NA (surface)", "Internal segment"]},
)
fig.update_layout(xaxis_tickangle=-40, height=480)
fig.show()
```

### Internal diversity vs. stem divergence (per clade)

```{python}
#| label: fig-clade-scatter

fig = px.scatter(
    trees_det,
    x="stem_bl",
    y="internal_bl",
    size="n_tips",
    color="type",
    hover_data=["tree", "clade_id", "n_tips", "mean_tip_dist"],
    color_discrete_sequence=PALETTE,
    labels={
        "stem_bl":     "Stem branch length (divergence from Africa)",
        "internal_bl": "Internal branch length (within-clade diversity)",
        "n_tips":      "Clade size",
        "type":        "Segment type",
    },
    title="Within-clade diversity vs. divergence from Africa — per clade",
    size_max=25,
    opacity=0.7,
)
fig.update_layout(height=520)
fig.show()
```

### Conclusion

| Pattern | Subtypes | Interpretation |
|---------|----------|----------------|
| 1–2 large clades (mean 14–35 tips), stem BL 0.018–0.031 | **H6N2, H9N2** (avian) | One or two founder events established long-lasting local lineages; sequences are genuinely distinct from the continental pool |
| 31–43 small clades (mean 4–10 tips), stem BL 0.001–0.002, ~20% singletons | **H1N1, H3N2, Flu B** (seasonal human) | Repeated annual reintroductions; chains die out quickly, no sustained local lineage |
| 51–71 clades (mean 6–10 tips), stem BL 0.001–0.002 | **Internal segments** (PB2, PB1, PA, NP, MP, NS) | Same short-lived introduction pattern as seasonal viruses; high clade count also partly reflects reassortment (internal genes travel with different surface proteins) |

**Take-away:** H6N2 and H9N2 are the only subtypes with evidence of established, locally-evolving lineages in Madagascar. Seasonal human viruses (H1N1, H3N2, Flu B) show no local persistence — they are re-imported every season, as in most of the world. Sustained Madagascar surveillance is justified specifically to monitor the avian reservoir (H6N2, H9N2), which is invisible to any other African country's programme.

## H9N2 reassortment analysis

H9N2 has established at least one long-lived Malagasy lineage. Influenza reassorts readily — surface proteins (HA, NA) can be exchanged for internal segments from co-circulating viruses. We therefore ask, for each Malagasy H9N2 isolate: does its internal segment complement also cluster within a Malagasy clade, or were those segments recently imported?

**Method:** `scripts/analyse_reassortment.py` reads `results/clade_tips.tsv` and, for every H9N2 Madagascar tip, determines whether each of the 8 segments is (a) in-clade with other Malagasy sequences, (b) a singleton (present in the tree but phylogenetically isolated), or (c) absent (segment not sequenced / not in the tree). Classification uses only present segments; "absent" is treated as missing data, not as evidence of importation.

```{python}
#| label: fig-reassortment-heatmap
#| fig-cap: "Segment × isolate heatmap for 71 H9N2 Madagascar isolates. Green = in Malagasy clade; grey = not sequenced; red = singleton (would indicate recent importation). Isolates sorted by year."

SEG_ORDER = ["HA", "NA", "PB2", "PB1", "PA", "NP", "MP", "NS"]
COLOUR_MAP = {"clade": "#2ca25f", "absent": "#d9d9d9", "singleton": "#e34a33"}

# Sort isolates by year (Norosoa year extracted from isolate_key)
def extract_year(key):
    try:
        return int(key.split("/")[-1].split("_")[0])
    except Exception:
        return 9999

mat = reassort_mat.copy()
mat["_year"] = mat["isolate_key"].apply(extract_year)
mat = mat.sort_values(["_year", "Host", "isolate_key"]).reset_index(drop=True)
mat["label"] = mat["isolate_key"].str.split("/").str[-1] + " (" + mat["Host"].fillna("?") + ")"

# Build a numeric Z matrix and hover text
status_to_z = {"clade": 1, "absent": 0, "singleton": -1}
Z    = [[status_to_z.get(mat.at[i, seg], 0) for seg in SEG_ORDER] for i in mat.index]
text = [[mat.at[i, seg] for seg in SEG_ORDER] for i in mat.index]

import plotly.graph_objects as go
fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
    z=list(zip(*Z)),          # transpose: rows=segs, cols=isolates
    x=mat["label"].tolist(),
    y=SEG_ORDER,
    text=list(zip(*text)),
    hovertemplate="Isolate: %{x}<br>Segment: %{y}<br>Status: %{text}<extra></extra>",
    colorscale=[
        [0.0,  COLOUR_MAP["singleton"]],
        [0.5,  COLOUR_MAP["absent"]],
        [1.0,  COLOUR_MAP["clade"]],
    ],
    zmin=-1, zmax=1,
    showscale=False,
))
fig.update_layout(
    height=350,
    xaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=8), tickangle=45),
    yaxis=dict(tickfont=dict(size=11)),
    margin=dict(l=60, r=20, t=30, b=120),
)
fig.show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-reassortment-summary
#| tbl-cap: "H9N2 Madagascar isolates by year, host, and reassortment classification."

from IPython.display import display
display(
    reassort_sum.rename(columns={
        "Year": "Year", "Host": "Host",
        "classification": "Classification", "n_isolates": "N"
    }).style.hide(axis="index")
)
```

**Result:** Across all 71 H9N2 Madagascar isolates (18 GISAID + 53 Norosoa), **zero singletons** were detected in any segment tree. Every sequenced segment clusters within a monophyletic Malagasy clade.  
This means the H9N2 lineage in Madagascar is genetically self-contained: all eight genome segments have been co-circulating locally since introduction, with no phylogenetically detectable replacement from continental strains. The predominant classification is `fully_local` (≥5 segments in-clade; 49/71 isolates) or `largely_local` (fewer segments sequenced but all in-clade; 22/71). No `reassorted` isolate was detected.

## Tree images

Tips: Madagascar = red, Africa = grey. Blue boxes = monophyletic Madagascar clades (`n=` size, `s=` stem BL).

```{python}
#| label: fig-tree-gallery
import base64
from IPython.display import display, HTML
imgs = sorted(TREES_IMG.glob("*.png"))
if imgs:
    parts = []
    for p in imgs:
        b64 = base64.b64encode(p.read_bytes()).decode()
        parts.append(
            f'<div style="display:inline-block;margin:6px;vertical-align:top">'
            f'<p style="font-size:11px;text-align:center;margin:2px 0">{p.stem}</p>'
            f'<img src="data:image/png;base64,{b64}" style="width:600px">'
            f'</div>'
        )
    display(HTML('<div style="line-height:1">' + "".join(parts) + '</div>'))
else:
    display(HTML('<p style="color:grey">No PNGs found in trees_img/ — run <code>scripts/render_trees.py</code> first.</p>'))
```
